title: Market Brief — Tuesday, June 9, 2026
summary: GPU oversupply is the story. Util-avail sits at 34% for A100, 42% for H100, 64% for H200, and 63% for B200, meaning next-gen silicon is flooding the market faster than demand absorbs it. H100 spot jumped 10.3% day-over-day to $2.58/hr against collapsing utilization down 41pp in 30 days, a classic short squeeze in a loosening market that will not hold.
paragraphs: GPU oversupply is the story. Util-avail sits at 34% for A100, 42% for H100, 64% for H200, and 63% for B200, meaning next-gen silicon is flooding the market faster than demand absorbs it. H100 spot jumped 10.3% day-over-day to $2.58/hr against collapsing utilization down 41pp in 30 days, a classic short squeeze in a loosening market that will not hold.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), serving Llama 70B on H100 runs $9.41/M tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M are selling at massive losses, burning roughly $9/M tokens each to buy market share. Grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Neutral, Google Strong, DeepSeek Burning Cash, open-source sub-dollar providers Burning Cash.

B200 at $4.58/hr spot with 63% util-avail and TCO-adjusted inference cost of $3.35/M is the only GPU class where medium-tier pricing breaks even, making it undervalued for new capacity commitments. H100 at $2.58/hr after a 10.3% spike with utilization cratering 41pp is overvalued; short H100 spot exposure and rotate into B200 contracts.
bullets: 64% util-avail on H200 signals clear oversupply in the highest-demand accelerator class.
57.9% H100 utilization today is down 41.2 percentage points in 30 days, the steepest demand collapse across all GPU tiers.
$80/M output for o3 Pro makes it 235x more expensive than OpenAI's own gpt-oss-120b at $0.34/M, the widest intra-provider spread tracked.
21.78 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token prices have fallen 78% from the GPT-4 launch baseline of 100.
$4.66/hr B200 OCPI carries 94.6% annualized volatility, signaling the market has not yet found a clearing price.
74% of advertised B200 offerings sit unrented on NeoCloud platforms, confirming physical surplus despite strong on-paper demand narratives.
